tools.name: get_app_intro
tools.description: Friendly introduction to OpenJob for job seekers. Use when the user is new or asks how this works. Return plain language only.

tools.name: explain_filters
tools.description: Plain-language search tips for job seekers. Use when the user asks how to search or what they can filter. No technical jargon in the reply.

tools.name: explain_data_source
tools.description: Where job listings come from, licensing, and links to the data provider. Use only when the user asks about data source, Feashliaa, license, attribution, or who built OpenJob.

tools.name: get_index_status
tools.description: How much of the job board is loaded and whether search is ready. Explain to the user in simple terms, not technical sync jargon.

tools.name: search_jobs
tools.description: Search open company job listings. Map the user's natural language to filters. Always set search_mode on search_jobs and get_job_details: keyword_search when the user only describes a role, place, or filters; resume_match when they upload a resume/CV or ask to match jobs to their background or memory

tools.name: get_job_details
tools.description: Fetch full posting text for job urls from search_jobs. Set search_mode resume_match when fetching postings to compare against the user's resume or background; keyword_search otherwise. Greenhouse (including embedded careers pages), Ashby, and Lever are supported; other career sites may return unsupp

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "search_jobs" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Search open company job listings. Map the user's natural language to filters. Always set search_mode on search_jobs and get_job_details: keyword_search when the…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "search_jobs" is 2739 chars, far longer than this server's median (157). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Search open company job listings. Map the user's natural language to filters. Always set search_mode on search_jobs and get_job_details: keyword_search when the…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "search_jobs" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Search open company job listings. Map the user's natural language to filters. Always set search_mode on search_jobs and get_job_details: keyword_search when the…

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "openjobs-3168f222.alpic.live" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: search_jobs, get_job_details · Public sink: search_jobs · Destructive: search_jobs.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: search_jobs, get_job_details · Public sink: search_jobs · Destructive: search_jobs

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
